benefitsPage.title: Your Benefits at a Glance
benefitsPage.description: Explore the complete range of benefits available to RS&H employees.

benefitChapters.title: Overview of Available Plans
benefitChapters.description: Overview of all available benefit plans for RS&H employees.

children.text: Review and compare all benefit plans available to RS&H employees.
Medical Plans

Your medical benefits are provided by BlueCross BlueShield and include coverage for both in-network and out-of-network providers. All plans participate in the same network, and you may choose your provider. Choosing a provider that is in network provides significant savings.
3 medical plans available: PPO Plan, Prime HDHP, Alternate HDHP
Dental Plans

Dental plans cover diagnostic and preventive care, plus basic and major services. You can choose any dental provider. Lower prices, quality assurance, no balance billing, and no pre-payment when using MetLife Plus dentists.
2 dental plans available: Core Plan and Enhanced Plan
Vision Plans

Vision care benefits include coverage for eye exams, lenses and frames, contact lenses, and discounts for laser surgery. The vision plan is built around network providers offering higher benefits at a lower cost. Out-of-network services will be reimbursed per the stated grid.
2 vision plans available: Core VSP and Enhanced VSP

tables.tableDescription: Bi-weekly associate payroll contributions for medical coverage.
Bi-weekly associate payroll contributions for dental coverage.
Bi-weekly associate payroll contributions for vision coverage.

benefitChapters.title: Eligibility & Qualifying Life Events
benefitChapters.description: Information about who is eligible for benefits, eligible dependents, enrollment, and qualifying life events.

children.text: Benefits are available to all full-time part-time associates (minimum 20 hours per week) and their dependents. If you enroll during Open Enrollment, your benefits will become effective on January 1, 2026. If you are newly hired, your benefits will become effective on the first day of the month following your date of hire.
Eligibility

Benefits are available to all full-time part-time associates (minimum 20 hours per week) and their dependents. If you enroll during Open Enrollment, your benefits will become effective on January 1, 2026. If you are newly hired, your benefits will become effective on the first day of the month following your date of hire.
Full-time or part-time associates (minimum 20 hours/week)
Enrollment during Open Enrollment or within 10 days of hire for new hires
Must notify within 30 days for qualifying life events
Eligible Dependents
Spouse or Domestic Partner
Children from birth to age 26 including natural, legally adopted, stepchildren, unmarried disabled children of any age dependent on you
Enrollment
Enroll during Open Enrollment annually
New hires must enroll within 10 days of hire
Changes due to Qualifying Life Events must be made within 30 days of event
Proof of qualifying life event is required
Changes must be consistent with event status
Common Qualifying Events
Changes in your life may permit you to update your coverage outside of open enrollment.
Changes to coverage must be made within 30 days of the life event. Proof of the life event is required.
Birth, legal adoption, or placement for adoption
Change in marital status
Dependent child reaches age 26
Spouse gains or loses employment or eligibility with current employer
Death of a covered dependent
Spouse or dependent becomes eligible or ineligible for Medicare/Medicaid or SCHIP
Change in residence that changes coverage eligibility
Court-ordered change

benefitChapters.title: PPO Plan Medical Plan
benefitChapters.description: Not HSA Eligible medical plan with in-network and out-of-network coverage.

children.text: Not HSA Eligible medical plan with in-network and out-of-network coverage.
Copays
Copayments are fixed dollar amounts for certain services.
PPO copays include $30 for primary care office visits, $55 for specialist visits, $250 copay per imaging scan (MRI, CT, PET), $75 copay for urgent care, and $500 copay for emergency room visits.
Deductible
Annual deductible: $2,000 per person in-network, $6,000 family maximum in-network
Out-of-network deductible: $4,500 individual, $13,500 family
Out-of-Pocket Maximum
Out-of-pocket maximum: $6,000 per person in-network, $12,000 family maximum in-network
Out-of-network out-of-pocket maximum: $9,000 individual, $18,000 family
In-network vs Out-of-network
**In-network**
In-network providers have agreements with your medical plan provider offering negotiated rates
Using in-network providers saves you money
**Out-of-network**
Out-of-network providers do not have agreements with your medical plan provider
You generally pay more and may be subject to balance billing (difference between full rate charged and insurance coverage)
Check if a provider is in-network by using the provider search tools on your plan's website or contact member services.
Help with Selecting Your Medical Coverage
ALEX is the official RS&H benefits counselor who provides personalized benefits guidance to help you select the best coverage.

Pharmacy benefits include preventive medication coverage, multiple retail tiers with copays, and mail order options with copays. Use in-network pharmacies and consider generic and mail order options for savings.
Teladoc Virtual Visits are available at no cost in PPO Plan; in HDHP plans costs apply after deductible.
**Toolkit App**: MyHealthToolkit app available for managing healthcare information and tools

tables.tableDescription: Bi-weekly associate payroll contributions for PPO Plan coverage

Prescription costs are determined by the tier assigned to each specific prescription drug.

benefitChapters.title: Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
benefitChapters.description: RS&H offers all associates and their families access to a confidential Employee Assistance Program (EAP) tailored to your needs. Health Advocate services are available 24/7 with licensed professionals

children.text: RS&H offers all associates and their families access to a confidential Employee Assistance Program (EAP) tailored to your needs. Health Advocate services are available 24/7 with licensed professionals for immediate assistance. The EAP helps with stress, depression, substance abuse, and other life challenges.
Services are available 24/7 via telephone, face-to-face, or web-based assistance.
Overview

RS&H offers all associates and their families access to a confidential Employee Assistance Program (EAP) tailored to your needs. Health Advocate services are available 24/7 with licensed professionals for immediate assistance. The EAP helps with stress, depression, substance abuse, and other life challenges.
Free Visits
Six free visits (virtual or in-person) with licensed professionals per incident.
Services include:
adoption
alcohol/substance abuse
anxiety
cancer support
child and elder care
diabetes
dieting
divorce and child custody
financial planning (estate, retirement, investing)
grieving
heart health
legal services
marriage and living together
military life
post-traumatic stress disorder
pregnancy
smoking
student life
wills
and more

benefitChapters.title: Health Savings Account (HSA) & Flexible Spending Account (FSA)
benefitChapters.description: HSA and FSA account options for tax-advantaged savings.

children.text: Understand your tax-advantaged savings account options.
What is a Health Savings Account?

A Health Savings Account (HSA) is designed to be paired with a qualified High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP), like RS&H Prime or Alternate HDHP, to provide a smart way to save for current and future healthcare needs.
Benefits of HSA
Contributions are tax-free
Any interest earned on the account is tax-free
Withdrawals for qualified health expenses are tax-free
You can use the account to pay for qualified healthcare expenses
Unspent dollars roll over each year and are yours to keep, even if you retire or leave the company
You can invest your HSA funds to grow available healthcare dollars over time.
Who is eligible for an HSA?
You are enrolled in the HDHP.
You are not covered by a spouse’s plan.
No one else can claim you as a dependent.
You are not enrolled in Medicare, TRICARE, or TRICARE for Life.
You have not received VA benefits in the past 3 months.
How to manage your HSA account
Access and manage your HSA through your Fidelity NetBenefit account at NetBenefits.com.
Establish your contribution amount during enrollment; you can change anytime throughout the year via benefit portal.
What is a Flexible Spending Account?

A flexible spending account (FSA) is a tax-advantaged account that can reimburse you for qualified healthcare or dependent care expenses. You can fund qualified expenses with pre-tax dollars deducted from your paycheck. For HDHP Prime & Alternate medical plan enrollees, your Limited Purpose Health Care FSA may only be used for eligible dental and vision expenses. Set annual contribution amount. Eligible claims must be incurred between January 1 and December 31. All claims must be submitted by March 15. Up to $660 of unused funds from the prior year may roll over. Unused amounts above $660 are forfeited.
Understanding different types of FSAs
For HDHP Prime & HDHP Alternate medical plan enrollees, Limited Purpose FSA is for dental and vision only.
Transit FSA
A Transit FSA allows you to use pre-tax dollars to pay for transit expenses for commuting (train, bus, ferry).
Expenses like tunnel, bridge, or highway tolls are ineligible.
Up to $340 per month contribution allowed, excess balances carried over to next month.
Can purchase additional passes above $340 monthly cost on a post-tax payroll deduction basis.
Parking FSA
A Parking FSA allows payment for eligible work-related parking expenses with pre-tax dollars, such as ramp or park-and-ride.

rows.label: Plan Eligibility & Population
cells: Associates enrolled in the PPO medical plan or waived medical coverage
HDHP Prime & HDHP Alternate medical plan enrollees
Available to associates with qualifying dependents

rows.label: Description
cells: Healthcare FSA reimburses for eligible medical expenses
Limited-Purpose FSA can be used for dental and vision care expenses
Dependent Care FSA reimburses care expenses for qualifying dependents such as children under 13 or elderly parents
Expenses incurred for a pass, token, fare card, voucher, or similar item for transportation on mass transit facilities
Tunnel, bridge, or highway tolls (EZ Pass)
Contribute up to $340 per month in pre-tax benefits for 2026, excess balances will be carried over into the following month
Non-work related transportation or parking expenses
If your pass needs exceed $340 per month, you may purchase these on a post-tax basis through convenient paycheck deductions.

benefitChapters.title: Survivor Benefits
benefitChapters.description: RS&H provides Basic Life Insurance and AD&D automatically to eligible employees equal to annual salary up to $1,000,000. Employees must designate beneficiaries. Coverage and amount reduce starting age

children.text: RS&H provides Basic Life Insurance and AD&D automatically to eligible employees equal to annual salary up to $1,000,000. Employees must designate beneficiaries. Coverage and amount reduce starting age 65.
RS&H provides Basic Life and Accidental Death & Dismemberment Insurance (AD&D)

RS&H provides Basic Life Insurance and AD&D automatically to eligible employees equal to annual salary up to $1,000,000. Employees must designate beneficiaries. Coverage and amount reduce starting age 65.
Coverage amount
Basic Life and AD&D amount equals annual salary up to $1,000,000
At age 65, policy reduces by 35%; at age 70 by 45%; at age 75 by 70%; at age 80 by 80%
AD&D Coverage
AD&D insurance pays an additional benefit equal to basic life insurance in the case of death by accident or pays partial benefits for dismemberment.
Death caused by accident results in AD&D amount paid in addition to life insurance
Dismemberment due to accident pays percentage of total AD&D benefit depending on body part/function lost
Other Voluntary benefits
Additional voluntary benefits include voluntary life and AD&D insurance for employee, spouse, and dependents with guaranteed issue amounts and evidence of insurability requirements.
Voluntary life insurance increments for associate up to $450,000 with $250,000 guaranteed issue
Spouse voluntary life insurance increments up to $225,000 with $50,000 guaranteed issue
Voluntary child life insurance increments up to $10,000 (no guarantee limit)
Voluntary AD&D insurance available with increments of $25,000 up to $500,000 maximum
Family coverage percentages vary depending on spouse/child coverage combinations
Designating Beneficiaries
You must designate a beneficiary.

benefitChapters.title: Supplemental Health
benefitChapters.description: Supplemental health benefits provided by Mutual of Omaha.

children.text: Provider: Mutual of Omaha
What is Supplemental Health?
Supplemental health plans work alongside your medical insurance to fill financial gaps when unexpected events occur.
Critical Illness Coverage
Coverage available with no medical questions
Family coverage included, children covered at no additional cost
$50 Health Screening Benefit per person per year
Pre-existing condition limitations only apply to cancer
Covered illness include:
Cancer
Heart Attack
Stroke
Major Organ Transplant
End Stage Renal Failure
Coronary Bypass Surgery (pays at 25%)
Advanced Parkinson's
Advanced Alzheimer's
Childhood and Developmental conditions
and MORE!
Payment frequency
Different diagnoses pay per illness with 6 months separation
Same diagnosis pays full amount twice with 6 months between
Accident Coverage
Family coverage available
Medical questions never required
Coverage pays per covered accident; unlimited accidents covered
Policy portable if you retire or leave RS&H

rows.label: Health Screening Benefit (Per Person, Per Year)

benefitChapters.title: Income Protection (Disability)
benefitChapters.description: Short-term and long-term disability insurance and income protection benefits.

children.text: RS&H offers both Short-Term Disability (STD) and Long-Term Disability (LTD) for income protection.
Understand your disability insurance options.
Overview
RS&H offers both Short-Term Disability (STD) and Long-Term Disability (LTD) for income protection.
Short-Term Disability Insurance Core Plan
Core plan provided at no cost by RS&H; enrollment automatic
Buy-Up plan available for purchase for extra protection
STD Notes
Pre-existing exclusion: No coverage for disabilities in first six months if treatment occurred in the prior three months.
Long term disability insurance
Provided by RS&H at no cost to you

rows.label: Your cost
cells: Provided by RS&H at no cost to you

cells: After 7 calendar days of inability to work
After 7 calendar days of inability to work

rows.label: How much it pays

An additional 30% of your income to a maximum of $2,000 per week

rows.label: Your cost

rows.label: How much it pays

benefitChapters.title: Financial Wellbeing (401k)
benefitChapters.description: The RS&H 401(k) plan allows you to contribute part of your salary toward retirement while deferring taxes.

children.text: The RS&H 401(k) plan allows you to contribute part of your salary toward retirement while deferring taxes.
Eligibility
All Associates over the age of 18, including temporary and part-time, are eligible to participate in the 401(k) plan.
Employee enrollment and contributions
New associates will be automatically enrolled at 6% pre-tax in the qualified default investment option, the T. Rowe Price Target Date Retirement Funds, with option to opt out within approx 45 days.
Automatically enrolled at 6% pre-tax.
Defaulted into T. Rowe Price Target Date Retirement Funds appropriate to age.
Opt-out or change deferral amount within 45 days from hire date.
Matching Contribution
RS&H matches 50% of your contributions up to 6% of pay.
Match is divided 2/3 cash and 1/3 company stock annually.
An associate earning $75,000 contributing 6% ($4,500) is matched $2,250; $1,500 cash and $750 company stock.
Vesting schedule
Your own contributions and growth are 100% vested.
Company match vests over 5 years, becoming 100% vested at 5 years.
Beneficiaries
Add and manage 401(k) beneficiaries directly on netbenefits.com; RS&H systems do not store this info.
Loans
Loans available if you are active with vested balance of $1,000+; request via netbenefits.com or Fidelity Net Benefits Line.
Employee Ownership
Employee ownership at RS&H includes 401(k) stock match and supplemental contributions, annual stock offerings, and incentive programs.
Opportunities for Investment
Opportunities for associates to invest and participate in company stock and equity.

rows.label: Standard Contribution
cells: Automatically enrolled at 6%. Contribute to traditional 401(k), Roth 401(k), or both.

rows.label: Catchup Contribution
cells: Must elect to participate in catchup contribution. Over $145k earned prior year catchup contributions go into Roth automatically.

rows.label: Super Catchup Contribution
cells: Must elect to participate in super catchup contribution. Over $145k earned prior year catchup contributions go into Roth automatically.

rows.label: Mega Roth
cells: After max annual standard and catchup contribution, may save additional in after-tax Roth contribution, automatically converted to Roth IRA.

benefitChapters.title: Paid Time Off and Other Benefits
benefitChapters.description: RS&H recognizes eight holidays observed with paid time off for eligible associates.

children.text: RS&H recognizes eight holidays observed with paid time off for eligible associates.
Holidays
RS&H recognizes eight holidays observed with paid time off for eligible associates.
New Year's Day
Martin Luther King Jr. Day
Memorial Day
Independence Day
Labor Day
Thanksgiving
Day after Thanksgiving
Christmas Day
Floating Holiday
Provides one additional floating holiday day per accrual year to be used anytime during the year with supervisor approval.
Religious Holidays
Supports associates observing religious holidays outside observed holidays by using Vacation or Floating Holiday with prior supervisor notice.
Time Away
Vacation allowance based on tenure credited at date of hire and each January, prorated for part-time.
Accrual Logic
Full vacation credited at beginning of calendar year based on expected full year worked.
Prorated for new hires and part-time associates by actual hire date or hours worked.
Personal Leave (PL)
Personal Leave (PL) for personal illness, family emergencies, medical appointments, credited at start of year based on expected full year worked.
Leave Bank (LB)
Unused Personal Leave rolls into Leave Bank with max 240 hours, accessible for qualified Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) unpaid leaves only.
Paid Parental Leave (PPL)
Paid Parental Leave (PPL) provides up to three weeks of parental leave to care for and bond with newborn/adopted child.
Up to two weeks of paid leave per birth or adoption (including surrogacy), regardless of multiple births or adoptions.
Associates with accrued Leave Bank can use up to 40 hours (one week) extra, making up to three weeks total paid leave.
Bereavement Leave

Up to 3 days Bereavement Leave provided for qualifying events for immediate family members including spouse, domestic partner, child, parent, stepparent, siblings, grandparents, grandchildren, or relatives of spouse/domestic partner.
Jury Duty
Paid leave provided for jury duty based on standard working hours.
Other PTO Details
Flexible Work Arrangements

rows.label: New Hire Accrual Less than 1 year
